zh.Metz, Lorraine, Holy Roman Empire: 海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇洛斯林根梅斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de Metz於大約1115年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇洛斯林根梅斯時，她的父親Folmar V de Metz27歲，母親Matilda von Dagsburg21歲。她於1140年與Lodewijk I van Loon在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 凱澤斯勞特艾分巴賀結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1180年之前，埋葬在比利時利包格波格洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes de Metz于大约1115年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇洛斯林根梅斯时，她的父亲Folmar V de Metz27岁，母亲Matilda von Dagsburg21岁。她于1140年与Lodewijk I van Loon在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 凱澤斯勞特艾分巴賀结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1180年之前，埋葬在比利时利包格波格洛。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Metz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a short form of the female personal name Mechthild, composed of ancient Germanic maht ‘might, strength’ + hild ‘strife, battle’.

German, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Metz in Lorraine, which took its name from a Gaulish tribe, the Mediomatrici; in the name of the place this became abbreviated to Mettis, hence the modern name.
German: from Mätz, a short form of the personal name Matthias (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Mette
meanings: North German: from a pet form of the female personal name Mechthild (see Metz ).
North German: occupational name for a miller, from Middle Low German matte, mette ‘dry measure’.
French: topographic name from Old French mete ‘boundary stone, boundary’ (from Latin meta ‘bounded estate’).

variants.name: Metzer
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Metz (see   Metz  ).
Dutch: occupational name for a bricklayer, Middle Dutch metsere.

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Metze
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Gerhard, Gernot.
metronymic from a short form of the personal name Mechthild (see Metz 1).

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Metz was born about 1115, in Metz, Lorraine, Holy Roman Empire, her father, Folmar V de Metz, was 27 and her mother, Matilda von Dagsburg, was 21. She married Lodewijk I van Loon in 1140, in Erfenbach, Kaiserslautern, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. She died before 1180, and was buried in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgium.

fr.Roman, Yakut ASSR, Russia, Soviet Union: Roman, Yakutskaya ASSR, Russia, Union soviétique
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de Metz est née environ 1115, à Metz, Lorraine, Saint-Empire romain, son père, Folmar V de Metz, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Matilda von Dagsburg, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Lodewijk I van Loon en 1140, à Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée avant 1180, et a été enterrée à Borgloon, Limbourg, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes de Metz um 1115 in Metz, Lothringen, Heiliges Römisches Reich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Folmar V de Metz 27 und ihre Mutter Matilda von Dagsburg 21. Sie hat 1140 in Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Deutschland Lodewijk I van Loon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb vor 1180 und wurde in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Metz è nata il circa 1115 nel Metz, Lothringen, Heiliges Römisches Reich, sua padre, Folmar V de Metz, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Matilda von Dagsburg, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Lodewijk I van Loon nel 1140, nel Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì prima del 1180, e fu sepolta a Borgloon, Limburgo, Belgio.

ja.Metz, Lorraine, Holy Roman Empire: ヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチロスリンジェンメッツ
ja.lifeSummary: Agnes de Metzが約1115年にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチロスリンジェンメッツで生まれた時，父Folmar V de Metzは27歳，母Matilda von Dagsburgは21歳であった。1140年にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス ケイセルスラウテルンエルフエンバチでLodewijk I van Loonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1180年以前に死去し，ベルギーリンブルグボルグロオンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Metz(이)가 대략 1115년 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 로스링 멧즈에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Folmar V de Metz(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda von Dagsburg(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1140년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 카이세르슬로터 르펜바치에서 Lodewijk I van Loon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1180년 이전 사망했으며 벨기에 림버그 볼그루에 묻혔다.

pt.Loon, Belgium: Loon, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Metz nasceu aproximadamente 1115, em Metz, Lothringen, Heiliges Römisches Reich, seu pai, Folmar V de Metz, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Matilda von Dagsburg, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Lodewijk I van Loon em 1140, em Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 1180, e foi sepultada em Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica.

ro.Roman, Yakut ASSR, Russia, Soviet Union: Roman, Yakutskaya ASSR, Russia, Uniunea Sovietică

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Metz родилась около 1115 г. в местности мец, лотринген, хилигесь рoмищесь раич, её отцу по имени Folmar V de Metz было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda von Dagsburg, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lodewijk I van Loon в 1140 г. в местности эрфенбач, ландкрис кеисирслаутирн, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла перед 1180 г. и была похоронена в местности борглун, лимбург, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes de Metz nació aproximadamente 1115, en Metz, Lothringen, Heiliges Römisches Reich, su papá, Folmar V de Metz, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Matilda von Dagsburg, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Lodewijk I van Loon en 1140, en Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió antes de 1180, y fue sepultada en Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica.
